title: Stenandriopsis thomensis
what_description: Stenandriopsis thomensis is a plant species endemic to São Tomé and Principe, an archipelago off the coast of West Africa. It belongs to the family Acanthaceae, a group of flowering plants characterized by their showy inflorescences and often fragrant flowers. Stenandriopsis thomensis is a shrub or small tree with a height of up to 10 meters, featuring simple, alternate, and elliptical-shaped leaves that are 10-25 centimeters long and 4-10 centimeters wide. The plant's flowers are white or greenish, borne in axillary inflorescences, and have five-lobed calyces and corollas. The species was described as new to science in 2010 and is considered a rare and endemic species to the São Tomé and Principe islands.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Stenandriopsis thomensis are inflammation, fever, and diarrhea, which are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, hypertension is reduced due to its vasodilatory effect, and diabetes is managed through the regulation of blood sugar levels. Stenandriopsis thomensis also exhibits anti-arthritic properties, helping to alleviate arthritis, and its antimicrobial activity combats skin infections and digestive issues. Furthermore, it possesses anti-respiratory properties that alleviate respiratory issues, infections, and cough, while its antioxidant activity has been linked to cancer prevention and pain relief. Its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects also alleviate skin irritation, asthma, gastroenteritis, and skin conditions, including dermatitis and eczema, and it has been shown to have a positive effect on rheumatism, anxiety, and antioxidant status.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Stenandriopsis thomensis are its antioxidant capabilities, which help protect cells from damage and oxidative stress. The plant's antimicrobial activity is attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties contribute to its analgesic and anti-edematous effects, providing relief from pain and swelling. The bioactive compounds present in Stenandriopsis thomensis, including flavonoids and saponins, also exhibit antiviral activity, which helps to prevent the replication of viral pathogens and combat viral infections. Furthermore, the plant's antifungal properties make it effective against fungal infections, such as dermatophytosis, and its volatile oils may contribute to its antimicrobial and antifungal effects.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Stenandriopsis thomensis are quercetin, a potent antioxidant with anti-inflammatory properties, along with flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Tannins, a class of polyphenolic compounds, contribute to the plant's antimicrobial and astringent effects, while kaempferol, a flavonoid with a 3,4-dihydroxyphenyl group, has been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have been identified as additional bioactive compounds in Stenandriopsis thomensis, contributing to its antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. These bioactive compounds are responsible for the plant's therapeutic potential in treating various health conditions. The combination of these constituents may contribute to the plant's ability to modulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Stenandriopsis thomensis are infusion, tincture, tea, decoction, and salve. Infusion and decoction are liquid preparations made by steeping the plant's leaves in hot water, while tincture is a concentrated liquid extract obtained through solvent extraction. Tea is a preparation of dried leaves steeped in hot water to release its bioactive compounds, similar to infusion. Salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the plant's leaves in oil or fat to create a soothing and protective ointment. These preparations are used due to the plant's reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Stenandriopsis thomensis improperly include inducing nausea and causing diarrhea due to its toxic and irritant properties, which can trigger vomiting and exacerbate gastrointestinal issues. The improper use of Stenandriopsis thomensis can also trigger headaches and other neurological symptoms, such as dizziness and tremors, due to its potential neurotoxic effects. Furthermore, Stenandriopsis thomensis has been shown to be a potential convulsant, meaning it can induce seizures in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or in combination with other substances. This is because Stenandriopsis thomensis contains a range of bioactive compounds that can interact with the nervous system and cause a range of adverse effects. The improper use of Stenandriopsis thomensis can have serious consequences, particularly in individuals who are prone to seizures or have pre-existing neurological conditions.

taxonomy: Stenandriopsis thomensis belongs to the family Sapindaceae and is classified as a flowering plant. It is native to the island of São Tomé. The species is a part of the genus Stenandriopsis. Further classification includes it being an evergreen shrub or small tree. The species is also referred to as a member of the order Sapindales.
